_meta.authored_from: §15.3 (PII scrub key list, 7 items) + rsrch-s15-pii-scrubber §6.3 (key-to-implementation mapping), cross-service-contract 2026-05-25
_meta.author: ChrisPachulski
notes: Each of the 7 cases corresponds to exactly one §15.3 scrub obligation.
Input is a minimal Sentry ErrorEvent fragment (only the fields required to exercise the scrub path).
Output is the same fragment after piiScrub() runs. Fields not listed in input are not present in output.
REDACTED_FIELD_KEYS set (§15.3): plexAuthToken, verifiedPlexServerId, XTREAM_USERNAME, XTREAM_PASSWORD.
STREAM_TOKEN_RE: \bt=([^&\s"']+) — replaces the query-param value with REDACTED.
JWE_CIPHERTEXT_RE: eyJ[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,}\. — replaces the matched prefix+dot with REDACTED.
scrubAuthorizationHeader: replaces Authorization header value with [redacted].
scrubCookieHeader: replaces eex.session=<value> in Cookie header with eex.session=[redacted].
These 7 vectors are the ground truth for TS (D-row piiScrub), Python (recommender port, M2), Rust (M3), Swift (M2 EmeraldKit). All language ports must pass every case.

vectors.name: plex-auth-token-in-extra
vectors.description: §15.3 item 1: plexAuthToken field in event.extra must be replaced with REDACTED. Tests REDACTED_FIELD_KEYS key-match on the extra object.

vectors.name: verified-plex-server-id-in-extra
vectors.description: §15.3 item 2: verifiedPlexServerId field in event.extra must be replaced with REDACTED. Tests REDACTED_FIELD_KEYS key-match. Server ID is a UUID-like string that could correlate a user to a specific Plex install.

vectors.name: xtream-credentials-in-extra
vectors.description: §15.3 items 3+4: XTREAM_USERNAME and XTREAM_PASSWORD in event.extra must both be replaced. Tests that both keys in REDACTED_FIELD_KEYS fire in a single event. Xtream creds in a Sentry event context would expose IPTV provider credentials to the error tracker.

vectors.name: stream-grant-token-in-request-url
vectors.description: §15.3 item 5: stream-grant token in request.url query param t= must be replaced with REDACTED. STREAM_TOKEN_RE fires on the URL string. The token value after t= is the HMAC-protected stream grant — exposing it to Glitchtip would allow token replay by a Glitchtip operator.

vectors.name: bearer-token-in-authorization-header
vectors.description: §15.3 item 6: Authorization: Bearer <device-token> header must be replaced. scrubAuthorizationHeader fires on request.headers.authorization (case-insensitive match). A device JWE in the Authorization header exposed to Glitchtip would leak the encrypted device identity.

vectors.name: session-cookie-in-cookie-header
vectors.description: §15.3 item 6 (cookie branch): eex.session=<JWE> in Cookie header must have the value replaced. scrubCookieHeader replaces the value of eex.session= while preserving other cookie pairs. A JWE session cookie in Glitchtip would expose the encrypted session claims.

vectors.name: jwe-ciphertext-in-sql-param
vectors.description: §15.3 item 7: JWE ciphertext appearing as a SQL parameter value (e.g., stored as extra context on a DB error) must be replaced by JWE_CIPHERTEXT_RE. Pattern: eyJ[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,}\. matches the JWE header prefix and first dot. Replacement is REDACTED. The trailing dot is intentional per spec — the shape is visibly redacted.
